title: mappa cochinchinensis
intro: Mappa cochinchinensis, also known as the Gamboge plant, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, particularly in countries such as Cambodia, China, and Vietnam. The plant's medicinal properties include its high concentration of gamboge, a yellow resinous substance with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which is often used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Mappa cochinchinensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Mappa cochinchinensis is often used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is made into a tea or a powder to be taken orally, or used topically to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
